feed.title: SAP Community - Machine Learning
feed.subtitle: Machine Learning Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Queries on SAP LLM and Joule?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are looking to enable Joule. However before doing so, we want to understand more about following question. Please share your thoughts on these questions.

1. Which LLM model is SAP using? Is it homegrown or Other models like GPT, BERT or LLAMA?

2. How is SAP addressing the hallucination issues?

3. Any other terms and condition that SAP want Client to accept in order to use Gen AI/Joule?
4. Is same LLM used in various modules or different LLM used ? For example for Job description one LLM, For SF chatboat another LLM model?
5. What are the update cadence for the SAP LLM Product like schedule release or Adhoc release?

6 .Where does this LLM resides and size of custom SAP LLM?

7. Once Joule is enabled, can we turn On/Off depending on modules?

entry.title: How to use HANA-ML python package in a HANA HDI Container and how to assign correct priveledges?
entry.content:
Hi ,

we are struggling with the use of an HDI container for machine learning with the python hana-ml package in Python.

In a previous question https://answers.sap.com/questions/13984833/how-to-set-privileges-for-hana-machine-learning-in.html?childToView=13984942 a topic on missing priviledges was raised. The answer in the end was to use the user/pw combination from the sharedaccesskey in the service definition. However, when using this user it needs to be hard coded. How can we assign priviledges "CREATE ANY" to the user which can be read via the python service object in python ? This is a different user. Also in the documentation it is not recommended to give CREATE ANY to a user in HDI container.

How can this issue be solved ?

Or is machine learning with python in general not allowed / recommended in a DHDI container ? That would however significantly reduce the value of the python approach.

Regards

Marcus

entry.title: What transport to use from SAP Learning QA to PROD?
entry.content:
Hello,

I hope you are fine,

Is it possible to enter all the elements in SAP Learning QA and then take them to Productive? Is there any transportation?

Thank you so much,

Kind regards

entry.title: Integrate SAP Cash Application Cloud application with S/4 HANA 2022
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to Integrate SAP Cash Application Cloud application with S/4 HANA 2022. Please share any Standard SAP Link/SAP Note/Standard SAP Guide which contains technical details to do the same.

Thanks and Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP HANA PAL Search table error: "Column doesn't exist"
entry.content:
Hello Community! 😄

When I was working with SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (SAP HANA 2.0 SPS05), I noticed an error in the procedure _SYS_AFL.PAL_LINEAR_REGRESSION_PREDICT. I’m wondering if any of you also got it.

According to the documentation columns of the parameter table are: „PARAM_NAME”, „INT_VALUE”, „DOUBLE_VALUE” and „STRING_VALUE”. Indeed, a table with such columns usually works and results are calculated correctly:

However, sometimes I get an error "Column ':PARAM_NAME' does not exist":

The same error is raised when the procedure is called with Python hana_ml library, but the names of columns that “do not exist” are different (without colons):

ERROR:hana_ml.algorithms.pal.linear_model:(256, 'sql processing error: "KDYMEK"."(DO statement)": line 13 col 1 (at pos 1233): search table error: _SYS_AFL.AFLPAL:FORECASTWITHLR_ANY: [102] (range 1) TypeMismatch exception: Column \'PARAM_NAME\' does not exist')
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\linear_model.py", line 1069, in predict
self._call_pal_auto(conn,
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\pal_base.py", line 859, in _call_pal_auto 
self.execute_statement = call_pal_auto_with_hint(conn_context,       
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\pal_base.py", line 1365, in call_pal_auto_with_hint
 if try_exec(cur, sql, conn):
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\pal_base.py", line 1323, in try_exec      
cur.execute(sql)
hdbcli.dbapi.Error: (256, 'sql processing error: "KDYMEK"."(DO statement)": line 13 col 1 (at pos 1233): search table error: _SYS_AFL.AFLPAL:FORECASTWITHLR_ANY: [102] (range 1) TypeMismatch exception: Column \'PARAM_NAME\' does not exist')

Workaround:

  1. renaming columns of the parameter table: ":PARAM_NAME, ":INT_VALUE", ":DOUBLE_VALUE", ":STRING_VALUE" (with colons) – doesn’t work in Python,
  2. or running the code once again as the error isn’t raised every time.

Do you know what causes this problem and how I can avoid it?

entry.title: FedML SAP Datasphere & Databricks
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am interested in applying FedML to Datasphere data in Databricks and subsequently deploying the inference results back into Datasphere, while also deploying the Machine Learning Model to SAP Kyma. Before proceeding, I have several technical questions that I would like clarified:

1) When I call an SAP Datasphere View for consumption in Databricks using SQL, does the data remain solely in SAP Datasphere, or is it also replicated into Databricks via the "select" command?

2) Is the configuration for the DBConnection between SAP Datasphere and Databricks the same as the connection setup between Databricks and SAP Kyma?

3) Can inference be performed on the deployed model within SAP Kyma?

4) Apart from Azure, Amazon, and Google, are there any other hyperscalers supported by Databricks that I might be overlooking?

I am looking forward to your responses. Thank you in advance.

Best regards from Germany.

entry.title: Re: Using PAL Procedures embeded in SQL Script (anonymous...
entry.content:
Hi Dirk,

based on your PAL procedure example, here is a simple example using anonymous blocks, you don't have to declare the structure of the PAL procedure output:

 

 

do
begin
tv_data = SELECT * FROM PAL_MULTIVARSTAT_DATA_TBL;
tv_parms = SELECT * FROM #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL;
CALL "_SYS_AFL"."PAL_MULTIVARIATE_ANALYSIS"(:tv_data, :tv_parms, tv_out);
SELECT * FROM :tv_out;
END;

 

 

The constraint if structures require to be declared or not comes from SQL Script.

Please note, usage of the Python Machine Learning client might be helpful, as you can script in Python and capture the ad-hoc generated SQL for the PAL procedure or even utilize methods to generate procedure code / even design-time artifacts. Just as another helping note.

I hope this is helpful, otherwise please share more details.

Best regards,

Christoph

entry.title: AI Core - Model Training and Inference for Multi output with multi Input types
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m working on a complex machine learning project using SAP AI Core, and I’ve hit a roadblock. The goal is to train and deploy a model that handles multiple input types (both numerical and categorical) and generates multiple outputs, which also include numerical and categorical values.

Problem Statement

 

  1. Model Training:
    • I have tried using models such as DecisionTreeRegressor, DecisionTreeClassifier, MultiOutputClassifier, and MultiOutputRegressor.
    • For encoding non-numerical fields, I’ve used LabelEncoder, OneHotEncoder, and MultiLabelBinarizer.
    • The training process sometimes fails, particularly when dealing with complex encoding schemes and multi-output formats.
    • Model Inference:
      • When training is successful, inference often fails. The primary issues are:
        • Difficulty in parsing multi-output results.
        • Challenges in converting outputs back to their original non-numerical forms.
        • Mapping values correctly to their respective output fields.

Detailed Steps and Issues

 

  1. Encoding:
    • For categorical inputs, I’ve tried both LabelEncoder for label encoding and OneHotEncoder for one-hot encoding.
    • For outputs, especially multi-label ones, I’ve used MultiLabelBinarizer.
    • However, combining these encoders with multi-output models seems to introduce complexity, particularly in maintaining the correct mappings during inference.
  2. Model Training:
    • Example code:

from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor

from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

from sklearn.model_selection import KFold, train_test_split

from sklearn.inspection import permutation_importance

from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder

from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder

from sklearn.preprocessing import MultiLabelBinarizer

from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputClassifier

from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputRegressor

from sklearn.compose import make_column_transformer

# Assume X_train and y_train are preprocessed input and output datasets model =

clf = MultiOutputClassifier(DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=DT_MAX_DEPTH, random_state=31))  

    clf.fit(train_x, Y_encoded)

  • Issues:
  • Encoding-decoding mismatches.
  • Models not handling mixed input-output types effectively.

 

  1. Inference:
    • Example code:

query = dict(call_request.json)

    input_features = [ # list of values from request call

        query['Company_code'],

        query['Vendor'],

        query['Amount'],

        query['InvoiceDocumenttype'],

        query['Fiscalyear'],

    ]

 

# Convert to DataFrame to match the input structure of the encoder  

    input_df = pd.DataFrame([input_features], columns=['Company_code', 'Vendor', 'Amount', 'InvoiceDocumenttype', 'Fiscalyear'])  

 

    # Preprocess input_features using one-hot encoder  

    input_features_encoded = encoder_X.transform(input_df)

 

    # Make prediction  

    prediction = model.predict(input_features_encoded)  

 

    # Postprocess prediction to original labels  

    prediction = encoder_Y.inverse_transform(prediction)

    # Map the prediction to the field names

    fields = ['HKONT','KOSTL', 'Profitcenters', 'Paymentterms', 'Partnerbanktype', 'Taxcode']

    prediction_dict = dict(zip(fields, prediction[0]))

  • Issues:
    • Decoding predictions back to original categorical values.
    • Ensuring the predictions map correctly to their respective fields.

Questions

  • Has anyone successfully implemented a multi-input multi-output model that handles both numerical and categorical data?
  • Are there specific strategies or best practices for encoding and decoding in such scenarios?
  • Can you recommend any resources or examples that deal with similar complexities?

Any insights, suggestions, or references would be immensely helpful!

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: Vector Engines Overview
entry.content:
Well, this is not a question but my general understanding of Vector engine and my personal note of it.

Vector Engine

SAP previously used SAP HANA cloud as the database which was a multi-model database. Now with the release of vector engine (in sap Hana cloud) we can store the data in form of vector embeddings also.

Now also the database is multi-model. We have just added the vector engine to the database.

 

So, SAP already had these types of models like spatial data, graph type data, JSON etc. Now we have a vector embedding type of data also which is managed by the vector engine.

SAP does not only store these data or embeddings but also perform analytical computations with them to leverage fast query and provide contextual data to LLM’s.

 

We have a new data type to store these types of data namely -> REAL_VECTOR.

A constructor -> TO_REAL_VECTOR.

And two new distance calculating functions -> l2distance(), cosine_similarity().

L2Distance() calculates the Euclidean distance between the two vectors.

Cosine_similarity() is based on the angle the two vectors make with each other, More angle means more similarity.

These distance calculations are important as they help to calculate the relation between two vectors and how closely they match with each other.

 

We can directly query the vectors using normal SQL-like syntax from our application layer. Not just query but also perform in memory computations and also use the vectors for Retrieval Augmented Generation(RAG) to get the contextual data from the LLS’s used.

 

What is vector embeddings?

These are Mathematical data representations of any objects in a multidimensional vector space. These vector embeddings are generated by AI which are good with certain data types like maybe pdf or word.

It converts the unstructured data to a format that can be processed quickly.

Now that we have vector embeddings, these can be used to find the matching record using the distance in the vector space. All the related docs are kept at one place so the distance is less.

 

 

 

From this figure we can understand the whole use case.

We have uploaded some pdf files to the cloud database. The DB will create the chunks of texts and convert it into vector embeddings using AI and store it in vector space in the vector engine in HANA DB. Now this data is accessible throughout the DB.

A user comes and queries for the word “DATABASE”, this query is now converted to a vector itself and with the use of semantic search it queries the DB with the vector of our string. We might not have the exact word in our DB. Now this query is processed through the LLM model for giving the answer, as we don’t have database string in our DB. It will look for the closest thing possible logically based on the distance measurement technique. Therefore, fish is not considered but the string ‘TABLE’ is given as output.

Hence we have utilized the vector engine in order to send contextual data regarding our own DB data to the LLM to give accurate results with least hallucinations. This is the concept of Retrieval augmented generation also(RAG) which uses third-party data source apart from the dataset used to train the LLM.

entry.title: Additional Filters for C4C Ticket Categorization ML
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently, we have a scenario on Ticket Categorization Machine Learning. In this scenario, we have 2 separate teams/org. One team (Team 1) is using cs_team1@org.com for their incoming emails to create tickets. The other team (Team 2) uses support@org2.com for their customer support tickets.

In our rule, we are routing the respective generated tickets based on the channel we have set up for those email addresses. Both teams work as customer support agents but for different subsidiary brands of the company.

The question here is how do we train the ML and ensure that the categorization assigned is meant for Team 1 vs Team 2? We have issues that the emails for Team 2 are getting routed to the queue of Team 1 because of the auto-categorization.

Note: We also have routing rules enabled for categories. The last rule is for incoming emails.

Thanks in advance!

Marc

entry.title: 1MV Cash Application Machine Learning Model - How to get 5,000-10,000 cleared payments in one Model
entry.content:
Dear Sir or Madam,

The following question has arisen when using machine learning to post-process the bank statement.

This documentation https://me.sap.com/notes/0003001671 is pointing out that 5,000 -10,000 cleared payments must be available in order to train the ML model sufficiently.

To do this, we carry out the following steps:

  1. ML_CASH_APP_JOB_STATUS_DP and set status to “In Progress” (P_INPROG)
  2. ML_CASH_APP_DATA_POST to extract training data
  3. ML_CASH_APP_JOB_STATUS_DP and set status to “Complete” (P_COMPL) -> This will trigger training in cloud

The Model Manager for SAP Cash Application can now be found in the BTP Cockpit:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CASH_APPLICATION/9668f05979b644cd960f0d83ae4ed0aa/73fd86f7258b46bf84ef47f415af18fa.html?locale=en-US

When steps 1 to 3 are executed, a new inactive model is created. With regard to the processed items, the statistics show that not all cleared payments from the ERP are processed cumulatively. We can recognize this by the number of items when we run this training job every month. The number does not accumulate. In addition, each training run creates a new model that must then be activated.

How are we supposed to obtain an ML model that has generated 5,000-10,000 cleared payments if each training run only processes a part or new cleared payments and then generates a new model on this basis?

Who can explain this in detail?

Thank you very much
Sebastian

entry.title: CAP deployment error of AI mission
entry.content:
Dear guys,

I try to reproduce the mission "Develop a CAP-based (multitenant) application using GenAI and RAG" in my environment. (single tenant on cloud foundry)
During the deployment step I got this error message:

I had to remove the {org-id} from the xsuaa-app name in mta.yaml file because our org-id contains spaces and does not fit the naming conventions. I just added a string "-kps".

Any ideas or hints what needs to be done to get it working?

Br
Wolfgang

entry.title: AI/ML Functionality in SAP IBP Inventory Optimization
entry.content:
Hi All,

Would like to gain an insight on the AI/ML functionality in the Inventory Optimization module of SAP IBP if there are any. Any leads or any Help portal links would help. If any functionality exists will be trying to check the changes in the outputs when using AI for the Inventory Optimization run.

Thanks in Advance

Purrab

SAP Integrated Business Planning for inventory Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning

entry.title: Where to deploy RAG application for SAP Gen AI Hub
entry.content:
Hi,

What are the best practices recommended by SAP regarding hosting the RAG application that is used to provide context for the LLM model being setup in SAP Gen AI Hub?

In one of the SAP training sessions for SAP Generative AI Hub, the SAP trainers had introduced using Business Application Studio (BAS) as the development platform. For the RAG application that is being developed in BAS, must it be containerized through Docker and deployed it in Cloud Foundry to be consume by some other SAP services e.g. SAP Build App? Is there any alternative to this?

Thank you.

entry.title: Can I add rows to an existing SAP HANA table from a python dataframe?
entry.content: hana_ml.dataframe.create_dataframe_from_pandas() allows me to create a new SAP HANA table from the data of a python dataframe. However if that HANA table already exists and contains data, I just want to add more rows to it. Is that also possible? Or do I have to create a temporary table via create_dataframe_from_pandas and then move the rows to the real table?

entry.title: Incorrect path when activating an environment
entry.content:
Hello experts,
I am currently working through this very interesting blog post. However, when installing Python or activating the environment, I encounter the following error ‘The system cannot find the specified path’.

I would be very happy to receive help from the experts here.

Many thanks
Patric

entry.title: How can I parallelize the training of a HANA_ML MLPRegressor?
entry.content:
According to the documentation setting thread_ratio=1 means all available threads on SAP HANA will be used for training, but I can only see 5 threads of the scriptserver being active. This is exactly the same behavior as when training with the default value of thread_ratio=0. How can I speed up the training via parallelization?

from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.neural_network import MLPRegressor

mlp = MLPRegressor(hidden_layer_size=(layer1,layer2),
activation='relu',
output_activation='relu',
max_iter=iterations,
weight_init='normal',
training_style='batch',
normalization='z-transform',
thread_ratio=1)

mlp.fit(train_df, features=x_columns, label='y')

entry.title: Custom Field not getting updated
entry.content:
Custom field ethnicity/Race is configured to sync from EC into LMS through the connector, checking the archives in connector schedulling, the field does come from but when you go to the Label on the profile in LMS it is not showing. 

sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM3_VAL=OCCUPATIONAL_LEVEL
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM4_VAL=CHALLENGE_STATUS
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM5_VAL=ETHNICITY
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM7_VAL=RISK_LOSS
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM8_VAL=IMPACT_LOSS

@PL1    

@Harish_Vatsa 
 
SF-Dan

entry.title: Missing Execution ID in SAP AI Launchpad After Connecting AWS S3 Bucket via Object Store Secret
entry.content: I'm using SAP AI Launchpad. I created an Object Store Secret and connected it to my bucket in AWS, as it is a demo the S3 has admin role privilege in order tu access and has an access point, but in my dataset in SAP AI Launchpad, I don't see an execution ID. What can I do?

entry.title: Sci-Kit learn library in SAP Datasphere Data Flow
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I tried to add Python code into a Sap Datasphere Data Flow utilizing the sci-kit learn library. However I always get errors. Does anyone know if the library is available in the Dataflow Script Console or what alteratives I would have here?

Many thanks,

Moritz
